title: QUIC RFC 9000 ACK_ECN Frame Codec Work Item
summary: Carve direct positive, negative, deterministic fuzz, and BenchmarkDotNet Dry proof for ACK_ECN frame parsing and formatting without claiming broader endpoint ECN deployment.
planned_changes:
- Add focused architecture, work-item, and verification artifacts for the ACK_ECN frame-codec proof.
- Link the involved RFC 9000 ACK and ACK_ECN requirements to the focused artifact set while preserving the broad RFC9000-0001 trace.
- Fill empty ACK_ECN requirement homes for required type 0x03 counters, type-field admission, type/count mismatch rejection, varint encoding, and count preservation.
- Reuse the deterministic ACK frame fuzz proof for representative ACK/ACK_ECN round-trips and truncation rejection.
- Add permanent BenchmarkDotNet coverage for ACK_ECN parsing and formatting in the existing frame-codec benchmark suite.
- Preserve focused verification evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-ack-ecn-frame-codec/`.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 ACK/ACK_ECN requirement-home tests, run `QuicFrameCodecBenchmarks` with the Dry job, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-ack-ecn-frame-codec/`.
out_of_scope: Receiver-side access to IP ECN fields.
ECN count accumulation from received packets.
Sender-side ECN ACK-count validation beyond the existing separate proof.
Runtime ACK scheduling or ACK delay policy.
completion_notes: Completed as a proof and benchmark topoff over the existing ACK_ECN frame codec; no production behavior changed.
